req.title: System-Admin updates document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for the requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a document through the REST API.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, ANY
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_token():
    """JWT / session token that belongs to a system-admin user."""
    return "Bearer valid-system-admin-token"


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_token():
    """JWT / session token that belongs to a regular (non-admin) user."""
    return "Bearer valid-regular-user-token"


@pytest.fixture
def existing_document():
    """A document that already exists in the system."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "title": "Original Title",
        "content": "Original content.",
        "status": "draft",
        "owner": "user-001",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """A fully valid update payload containing all mandatory fields."""
    return {
        "title": "Updated Title",
        "content": "Updated content with new information.",
        "status": "published",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Mock for the audit-logging service."""
    with patch("app.services.audit_service.write_audit_record") as mock_audit:
        yield mock_audit


@pytest.fixture
def mock_document_repository(existing_document):
    """Mock for the document persistence layer."""
    with patch("app.repositories.document_repository.DocumentRepository") as MockRepo:
        instance = MockRepo.return_value
        instance.find_by_id.return_value = existing_document
        instance.update.return_value = {**existing_document, "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}
        yield instance


@pytest.fixture
def client(mock_document_repository, mock_audit_service):
    """
    Flask / FastAPI test client wired with mocked dependencies.
    Adjust the import to match your actual application factory.
    """
    from app import create_app  # adjust to your module path

    app = create_app(testing=True)
    with app.test_client() as test_client:
        yield test_client


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def put_document(client, document_id, payload, token):
    """Convenience wrapper for the update endpoint."""
    return client.put(
        f"/api/v1/documents/{document_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": token, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:

    def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """No token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = client.put(
            f"/api/v1/documents/{existing_document['id']}",
            json=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_invalid_token_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """Malformed / expired token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = put_document(
            client,
            existing_document["id"],
            valid_update_payload,
            token="Bearer invalid-or-expired-token",
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_non_admin_user_returns_403(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, non_admin_token
    ):
        """Authenticated but non-admin user → 403 Forbidden."""
        response = put_document(
            client,
            existing_document["id"],
            valid_update_payload,
            token=non_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN

    def test_system_admin_is_permitted(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        """System-admin with valid payload → 200 OK."""
        response = put_document(
            client,
            existing_document["id"],
            valid_update_payload,
            token=system_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Successful Update
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulUpdate:

    def test_response_status_is_200(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_response_contains_updated_fields(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert body["title"] == valid_update_payload["title"]
        assert body["content"] == valid_update_payload["content"]
        assert body["status"] == valid_update_payload["status"]

    def test_document_id_unchanged_after_update(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert body["id"] == existing_document["id"]

    def test_updated_at_timestamp_is_refreshed(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert body["updated_at"] != existing_document["updated_at"]

    def test_response_content_type_is_json(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert "application/json" in response.content_type


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["title", "content", "status"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_422(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """Each mandatory field, when absent, must cause 422 Unprocessable Entity."""
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["title", "content", "status"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_error_detail(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """Error body must identify which field is missing."""
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert missing_field in str(body).lower()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "field, bad_value",
        [
            ("title", ""),          # empty string
            ("title", "   "),       # whitespace only
            ("content", ""),        # empty string
            ("status", ""),         # empty string
            ("status", "unknown"),  # invalid enum value
            ("title", None),        # null
            ("content", None),      # null
        ],
    )
    def test_invalid_mandatory_field_value_returns_422(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        field,
        bad_value,
    ):
        """Mandatory fields with invalid values must be rejected."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, field: bad_value}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY

    def test_empty_body_returns_422(
        self, client, existing_document, system_admin_token
    ):
        """Sending an empty JSON object must be rejected."""
        response = put_document(client, existing_document["id"], {}, system_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY

    def test_non_json_body_returns_415(
        self, client, existing_document, system_admin_token
    ):
        """Non-JSON content type must be rejected with 415 Unsupported Media Type."""
        response = client.put(
            f"/api/v1/documents/{existing_document['id']}",
            data="title=foo&content=bar&status=draft",
            headers={
                "Authorization": system_admin_token,
                "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
            },
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Non-Existent & Edge-Case Resource Handling
# ===========================================================================

class TestResourceHandling:

    def test_update_nonexistent_document_returns_404(
        self,
        client,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_document_repository,
    ):
        """Updating a document that does not exist must return 404."""
        mock_document_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        response = put_document(
            client,
            str(uuid.uuid4()),  # random, non-existent ID
            valid_update_payload,
            system_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND

    def test_update_with_malformed_document_id_returns_400(
        self, client, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        """A syntactically invalid document ID must return 400 Bad Request."""
        response = put_document(
            client, "not-a-valid-uuid!!!", valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Audit Record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_update(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """A successful update must trigger exactly one audit record write."""
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        mock_audit_service.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_document_id(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("resource_id") == existing_document["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("action") == "UPDATE_DOCUMENT"

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_identity(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit entry must record who performed the action."""
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("performed_by") is not None
        assert call_kwargs.get("performed_by") != ""

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("timestamp") is not None

    def test_no_audit_record_written_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """A failed update (missing mandatory field) must NOT produce an audit record."""
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != "title"}
        put_document(client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token)
        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_record_written_on_auth_failure(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        non_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """An unauthorised request must NOT produce an audit record."""
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, non_admin_token
        )
        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_record_written_when_document_not_found(
        self,
        client,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
        mock_document_repository,
    ):
        """An update attempt on a non-existent document must NOT produce an audit record."""
        mock_document_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        put_document(
            client, str(uuid.uuid4()), valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_contains_changed_fields_snapshot(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit entry should capture what changed (before/after snapshot)."""
        put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
        )
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args.kwargs
        assert "before" in call_kwargs or "changes" in call_kwargs
        assert "after" in call_kwargs or "changes" in call_kwargs


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Idempotency & Concurrency Edge Cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestIdempotencyAndEdgeCases:

    def test_updating_with_same_values_returns_200(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        system_admin_token,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Re-sending the current values should still succeed (idempotent PUT)."""
        payload = {
            "title": existing_document["title"],
            "content": existing_document["content"],
            "status": existing_document["status"],
        }
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_extra_fields_in_payload_are_ignored(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
    ):
        """Unknown / extra fields in the payload must not cause an error."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "unexpected_field": "should-be-ignored"}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_extra_fields_are_not_persisted(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
    ):
        """Unknown fields in the payload must not appear in the response body."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "unexpected_field": "should-be-ignored"}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert "unexpected_field" not in body

    def test_title_at_maximum_allowed_length_is_accepted(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
    ):
        """A title exactly at the maximum allowed length must be accepted."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "A" * 255}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_title_exceeding_maximum_length_is_rejected(
        self,
        client,
        existing_document,
        valid_update_payload,
        system_admin_token,
    ):
        """A title exceeding the maximum allowed length must be rejected with 422."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "A" * 256}
        response = put_document(
            client, existing_document["id"], payload, system_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY
```

---

### Notes

| Area | What the tests verify |
|---|---|
| **Authentication** | Missing token → 401; invalid token → 401 |
| **Authorisation** | Non-admin → 403; system-admin → 200 |
| **Successful update** | 200 status, correct body fields, ID unchanged, `updated_at` refreshed, JSON content-type |
| **Mandatory field validation** | Each field absent → 422; invalid values (empty, whitespace, null, bad enum) → 422; empty body → 422; wrong content-type → 415 |
| **Resource handling** | Non-existent document → 404; malformed ID → 400 |
| **Audit record** | Written once on success; contains document ID, action type, actor, timestamp, and a before/after snapshot; **not** written on validation failure, auth failure, or 404 |
| **Edge cases** | Same-value PUT is idempotent; unknown payload fields are ignored and not persisted; boundary length checks |

Replace the `from app import create_app` import and field names with your actual application structure. If you are using FastAPI instead of Flask, swap `client.put(...)` for the `httpx`-based `TestClient` equivalent.

"""
Pytest tests for the requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a document through the REST API.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, ANY
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_token():
    """JWT / session token that belongs to a system-admin user."""
    return "Bearer valid-system-admin-token"


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_token():
    """JWT / session token that belongs to a regular (non-admin) user."""
    return "Bearer valid-regular-user-token"


@pytest.fixture
def existing_document():
    """A document that already exists in the system."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "title": "Original Title",
        "content": "Original content.",
        "status": "draft",
        "owner": "user-001",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """A fully valid update payload containing all mandatory fields."""
    return {
        "title": "Updated Title",
        "content": "Updated content with new information.",
        "status": "published",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Mock for the audit-logging service."""
    with patch("app.services.audit_service.write_audit_record") as mock_audit:
        yield mock_audit


@pytest.fixture
def mock_document_repository(existing_document):
    """Mock for the document persistence layer."""
    with patch("app.repositories.document_repository.DocumentRepository") as MockRepo:
        instance = MockRepo.return_value
        instance.find_by_id.return_value = existing_document
        instance.update.return_value = {**existing_document, "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}
        yield instance


@pytest.fixture
def client(mock_document_repository, mock_audit_service):
    """
    Flask / FastAPI test client wired with mocked dependencies.
    Adjust the import to match your actual application factory.
    """
    from app import create_app  # adjust to your module path

    app = create_app(testing=True)
    with app.test_client() as test_client:
        yield test_client


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def put_document(client, document_id, payload, token):
    """Convenience wrapper for the update endpoint."""
    return client.put(
        f"/api/v1/documents/{document_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": token, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:

    def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """No token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = client.put(
            f"/api/v1/documents/{existing_document['id']}",
            json=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_invalid_token_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_document, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """Malformed / expired token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = put_document(
            client,
            existing_document["id"],
            valid_update_payload,
            token="Bearer invalid-or-expired-token",
        )
        assert response.status_co
